authority: UAE Ministry of Finance (MoF) - central coordinating authority
legal_basis: Federal Law No. 54 of 2018 (ratifying MAC); Federal Law No. 48 of 2018 (ratifying MCAA); Cabinet Resolution No. 93 of 2021 (implementing provisions); Ministerial Resolution No. 134 of 2021
crs_status: Committed and active - CRS went live 1 January 2017. Multi-regulator jurisdiction.
reporting_deadline: 30 June following the 31 December reporting year-end [INFERRED - portal guidance says 'stipulated domestic reporting deadline'; verify via MoF portal announcement]
submission_method: UAE FATCA/CRS System at fatcacrs.mof.gov.ae; combined portal for CRS and FATCA; XML upload
xml_schema: OECD CRS XML Schema v2.0; MoF technical guidance in User Guide v2.4 (June 2026)
nil_report_required: Yes - required per UAE CRS Circular on Reporting (June 2026) [INFERRED - verify in circular text]
data_retention_years: 5 years minimum under UAE CRS regulations
msg_ref_id_format: AE + FI Tax Registration Number (TRN) + year + sequence
doc_ref_id_format: AE + year + FI TRN + unique account reference; per MoF User Guide v2.4
character_encoding: UTF-8; Arabic transliteration to Latin required for XML submission

adgm.authority: Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM) Financial Services Regulatory Authority (FSRA)
adgm.notes: ADGM-licensed entities report via ADGM FSRA, not directly to MoF. Separate ADGM CRS Regulations 2017 apply.

difc.authority: Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC) Authority / Dubai Financial Services Authority (DFSA)
difc.notes: DIFC-licensed entities are subject to DIFC Law No. 2 of 2018 and DIFC CRS Regulations 2020.

sca.notes: SCA-licensed securities FIs report under SCA Decision No. 21 of 2020.

local_fi_scope: UAE Central Bank licensed banks (conventional and Islamic)
Investment companies and brokers licensed by SCA
Any FI with financial accounts of non-UAE-resident customers
excluded_accounts: Qualified credit card issuer accounts (where applicable)
UAE government entity accounts (federal and emirate government accounts)
Accounts of international organisations with UAE offices
local_specific_rules: MULTI-REGULATOR STRUCTURE (CRITICAL): UAE has four separate regulatory tracks for CRS. An FI must identify its primary regulator: (1) Mainland UAE under CBUAE/MoF; (2) ADGM under ADGM FSRA; (3) DIFC under DFSA; (4) SCA for securities. Each regulator has separate CRS regulations and may have different procedural requirements. FIs must NOT assume mainland MoF rules apply to ADGM or DIFC entities.
COMBINED FATCA/CRS PORTAL: UAE uses a single system (fatcacrs.mof.gov.ae) for both CRS and FATCA submission. FIs must complete Risk Self-Assessment annually in addition to data reporting.
RISK SELF-ASSESSMENT: Required annually via the MoF portal system. FIs must assess and document their CRS compliance risk profile. Failure to complete Risk Self-Assessment is a compliance breach.

RESIDENCE BY INVESTMENT: UAE has a specific circular on Residence by Investment (RBI) and Citizenship by Investment (CBI) schemes. Account holders with UAE residence/citizenship obtained through such schemes may still be reportable to their original jurisdiction. FIs must apply due diligence accordingly.
UNDOCUMENTED ACCOUNTS CIRCULAR (JUNE 2026): MoF published a dedicated circular on undocumented accounts in June 2026. FIs must follow this for accounts where self-cert cannot be obtained.
ARABIC NAMES: Account holder names in Arabic must be transliterated to Latin characters for XML submission. FIs should use a consistent romanisation standard (e.g. ALA-LC).
TIN GUIDANCE (JUNE 2026): MoF published CRS Circular on TIN Guidance in June 2026 specifying how to handle missing TINs and which reason codes to use. Follow this circular.
USER GUIDE V2.4: MoF published FATCA/CRS System User Guide v2.4 in June 2026. This supersedes all earlier versions and should be followed for portal submission procedures.

ISLAMIC FINANCE PRODUCTS: UAE has a significant Islamic finance sector. Products such as murabaha accounts, sukuk holdings, and takaful contracts must be assessed for CRS scope. The MoF CRS Guidance Notes address Islamic finance account classification.

DIFC/ADGM SEPARATE LAWS: DIFC CRS Regulations 2020 and ADGM CRS Regulations 2017 (consolidated) are separate from mainland UAE CRS Regulations. DIFC/ADGM FIs should work with their respective regulations, not the mainland MoF rules.

rejection_error_codes.desc: FI not registered in UAE FATCA/CRS system
rejection_error_codes.resolution: Register at fatcacrs.mof.gov.ae using TRN before submission

rejection_error_codes.desc: Risk Self-Assessment not completed for current year
rejection_error_codes.resolution: Complete annual Risk Self-Assessment in portal before data submission
Validate against OECD CRS XSD and MoF User Guide v2.4 specifications
Use OECD2 with CorrDocRefId to amend
Use 15-digit UAE Tax Registration Number as FI identifier

penalties.failure_to_report: Under Cabinet Resolution 93/2021: administrative fines up to AED 300,000 per violation (INFERRED - verify in resolution text)
penalties.failure_to_register: Administrative penalty from MoF or relevant regulator
penalties.false_information: Potential criminal referral under UAE Penal Code

fatca.iga_effective: 1 July 2014 (retroactive effective date per IGA terms)
key_rules: UAE Model 1B (non-reciprocal): UAE FIs report FATCA data to MoF, which passes to IRS. UAE does NOT receive US tax information in return (non-reciprocal).
US person identification: 7 standard indicia. UAE-specific guidance in MoF FATCA Guidance Notes.
FATCA submission via same MoF portal as CRS.
FATCA User Guide v2.4 (June 2026) applies to FATCA portal procedures.
ADGM, DIFC, and SCA entities also subject to FATCA under their respective regulations.
US TIN Guidance circular (June 2026) published by MoF for handling missing US TINs.

sources.notes: UAE MoF AEOI page - fetched and verified. Page updated 18 June 2026. User Guide v2.4 confirmed.

gaps: Reporting deadline (30 June) is INFERRED; verify via MoF CRS Circular on Reporting (June 2026 PDF)
Penalty amounts under Cabinet Resolution 93/2021 should be confirmed from resolution text
ADGM and DIFC CRS regulatory deadline may differ from mainland deadline; check ADGM/DFSA guidance
